evidence: CLAUDE.md architecture section names 9 agents under a flat agents/ directory; find agents -name '*.md' | wc -l returns 77 files across 13 subdirectories

suggested_fix: Rewrite the architecture section to reflect the current 13-category directory structure and mention commands/ and root-level docs

evidence: agents/core/content-marketer-writer.md and agents/specialized-agents/content-marketer-writer.md both declare 'name: content-marketer-writer' in frontmatter
description: Two independently-authored agent files register under the identical name content-marketer-writer, creating an ambiguous/colliding registration
suggested_fix: Rename one of the two agents (e.g. to core-content-marketer-writer) to disambiguate

evidence: README.md 'Core Technical Excellence (14 agents)' section lists 14 bullet items; ls agents/core/ returns 15 files, with gpt-5.md absent from the README bullet list
description: README's Core Technical Excellence section undercounts agents/core/ by one file (gpt-5.md is undocumented)
suggested_fix: Add a bullet for gpt-5.md to the Core Technical Excellence section and update the count to 15

description: 76 of 77 agent files never declare a model: tier in frontmatter, indicating a repo-wide template gap rather than isolated oversights
suggested_fix: Add a model: field to the shared agent template and bulk-apply across all affected files

description: The finance-strategy/, ai-automation-specialists/, and growth-revenue-operations/ directories (20 files) share a near-identical boilerplate skeleton (single example block, DELIVERABLE STANDARDS section, no tools/model/output-format), consistent with one shared template rather than independent authorship
suggested_fix: Consider factoring the shared skeleton into a documented template and diversifying each agent's distinguishing sections

description: 76 of 77 agent files never declare a model tier in frontmatter (only agents/core/gpt-5.md declares one)
suggested_fix: Add model: sonnet (or an appropriate tier) to each agent's frontmatter

description: 54 of 77 agent files never declare a tools field in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add a tools: field listing the capabilities each agent actually needs

description: 36 of 77 agent files specify no output format or deliverable structure anywhere in the body

description: 17 files (5x finance-strategy, risk-assessor-fs, 5x ai-automation-specialists, 6x growth-revenue-operations) have exactly one example block in their description

evidence: grep for <example> in the affected files returns zero matches
description: 8 files (3x agents/core, 5x agents/marketing missing-frontmatter files) have zero example blocks in their description

description: Roughly 59 individual instances across ~20 files where a declared tool (Grep/Glob/MultiEdit/WebSearch/WebFetch/TodoWrite) is never referenced or exercised anywhere in the body
suggested_fix: Remove unused tools from each agent's tools: list, or add body content that exercises them

description: 33 files across all categories use vague quantifiers (appropriate, relevant, properly, various, etc.) without measurable criteria, penalty capped at -20 per file
suggested_fix: Replace vague quantifiers with concrete, measurable criteria

evidence: tools: field includes MultiEdit; body describes only analysis, trend identification, and report generation with zero implementation/edit verbs across ~270 lines
description: MultiEdit (an Edit-family tool) is declared on an agent whose entire role is read-only test-result analysis and reporting
suggested_fix: Remove MultiEdit from the tools: field

description: Command takes structured input (/workflow [request/PRD/PRP] [--flags]) but declares no argument-hint, and its Input Type Detection section never checks for the fully-empty-argument case
suggested_fix: Add an argument-hint field and an explicit empty-input branch to the Input Type Detection logic

description: CLAUDE.md has no runnable test-run command, is over 60% descriptive rather than instructive, and has no prerequisites/setup section
suggested_fix: Add a runnable test command, a prerequisites/setup section, and convert descriptive sections into actionable instructions
